channel.title: Neal Frazier — Stories
channel.description: Stories from the journey — building, learning, and solving real problems for real businesses.

item.title: The $100 One-Page Site That Gets New Businesses Moving Fast
item.description: Why the one-page launch offer exists: solve the "not online yet" bottleneck with a low-friction site that can start generating trust and leads immediately.

item.title: I Turned a Regular Laptop Into a Practical AI Command Center
item.description: My practical AI workstation model for operators: turn scattered subscriptions into a weekly execution system with clear task lanes and governance boundaries.

item.title: Copy That Converts: Reusing Blocks Without Sounding Generic
item.description: How I reuse high-performing copy frameworks without making businesses sound the same: same structure, different voice, proof, and local context.

item.title: The 7-Day Website Launch Sprint I Use for New VB Brands
item.description: Inside the 7-day launch sprint: a day-sequenced workflow that solves early-stage business urgency without skipping SEO, analytics, or conversion fundamentals.

item.title: From Click to Confirmed Booking in Under 90 Seconds
item.description: A booking funnel benchmark for local services: if first-time visitors cannot go from click to confirmation in 90 seconds, the funnel needs redesign.

item.title: My Journey to Becoming a Certified Netlify Partner
item.description: The practical reason behind my Netlify Partner path: solve inconsistent delivery quality with measurable deployment standards, not freelancer heroics.

item.title: Manifesto: From AI Shipper to Netlify Partner Architect
item.description: My operating manifesto: combine AI prototyping speed with partner-grade Netlify architecture so products can move from idea to reliable production without platform bloat.

item.title: Solving the 'Get Booked' Problem for VB Specialty Shops
item.description: How I fix the 'interested but not booked' gap for Virginia Beach specialty businesses by reducing booking friction to one path, one CTA, and one reliable handoff.

item.title: Why I Built 100 Websites in 30 Days (And Hosted Them on Netlify)
item.description: Why the 100-sites sprint was built as a credibility engine: one clear pain point (slow delivery trust), one repeatable production system, and one business outcome local clients can verify.
